---
title: "Tumor-only variant filtering for Brazilian cancer cohorts"
author:
  - name: "Mariana Boroni lab"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  BiocStyle::html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    code_folding: show
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Tumor-only variant filtering for Brazilian cancer cohorts}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6
)

project_root <- normalizePath(getwd(), mustWork = FALSE)
if (!dir.exists(file.path(project_root, "scripts"))) {
  candidate_root <- normalizePath(file.path(getwd(), ".."), mustWork = FALSE)
  if (dir.exists(file.path(candidate_root, "scripts"))) {
    project_root <- candidate_root
  }
}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_root)
```

# Overview

This vignette describes an R workflow for filtering tumor-only somatic variant
calls generated by Mutect2/DRAGEN/VEP-like pipelines, with emphasis on Brazilian
cancer cohorts where matched normals and a panel of normals (PoN) may not be
available.

The key design principle is to separate four questions:

1. Is the variant technically credible?
2. Is the variant more compatible with somatic origin, germline origin, or
   artifact?
3. If likely somatic, is it a driver candidate or likely passenger?
4. What sample-level summaries can be safely reported, such as QC, TMB,
   recurrence and approximate clonality?

Tumor-only calls should not be reported as confirmed somatic only because they
passed a filter. In this workflow, the final output is a prioritized
classification with explicit caveats, not a replacement for matched-normal
validation.

# Workflow summary

The pipeline takes a table with one row per variant per sample and writes
scored variant tables, sample-level QC summaries, TMB, driver/passenger
prioritization, clonal fraction estimates and visualization files.

```{r pipeline-figure, echo=FALSE, out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics(
  normalizePath(file.path(project_root, "figures", "pipeline_fluxograma.svg")),
  rel_path = FALSE
)
```

| Step | Output used by next step |
|---|---|
| Annotated variant table | Standardized sample, coordinate, gene and consequence fields |
| Technical feature extraction | `DP`, `ref_count`, `alt_count`, `VAF`, `TLOD`, `MBQ`, `MMQ` |
| Adaptive hard filters | `hard_filter_pass`, `hard_filter_reason`, sample QC class |
| Internal recurrence model | Pan-cohort and tumor-type recurrence features |
| Somatic/germline/artifact scoring | `somatic_score`, `germline_score`, `artifact_score` |
| OncoKB/COSMIC validation layer | External validation and rescue flags |
| Final classification | `final_class` |
| Driver/passenger scoring | `driver_class`, dNdScv and CHASMplus input tables |
| ML filter | `ml_true_positive_probability` and model status |
| TMB and clonality | Per-sample summaries |
| Visualization | MAF, oncoplots, QC, TMB, driver and clonality figures |

# Installation and dependencies

The workflow is written as plain R scripts. Required packages for the core
pipeline are:

```{r dependencies, eval=FALSE}
install.packages(c("data.table", "yaml", "ggplot2"))
```

Recommended optional packages:

```{r optional-dependencies, eval=FALSE}
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("BiocManager")
}

BiocManager::install(c("BiocStyle", "maftools"))
```

`BiocStyle` is used only to render this vignette in a Bioconductor-like format.
`maftools` is optional in the pipeline. If it is absent, the workflow still
writes a maftools-compatible MAF and a fallback oncoplot using `ggplot2`.

# Input data

The input table should contain one variant per row. The script accepts several
common column names and standardizes them internally.

Minimum recommended columns:

| Concept | Accepted examples |
|---|---|
| Sample | `Tumor_Sample_Barcode`, `Sample_Barcode`, `Sample`, `sample_id` |
| Coordinates | `CHROM`, `START`, `POS`, `REF`, `ALT` |
| Gene | `Hugo_Symbol`, `SYMBOL`, `Gene` |
| Consequence | `Consequence`, `Variant_Classification` |
| Depth and allele support | `DP`, `ref_count`, `alt_count`, `AD`, `AF`, `VAF` |
| Technical metrics | `TLOD`, `MBQ`, `MMQ`, `STRANDQ`, `FILTER` |
| Population AF | `GMAF`, `gnomADe_AF`, `gnomADg_AF`, `ABraOM_AF`, `BIPMed_AF` |
| Tumor type | `tumor_type`, `ONCOTREE_CODE`, `Cancer_Type`, `Tumor_Type` |

For Brazilian cohorts, population frequency columns are especially important.
Because Brazilian ancestry is admixed and underrepresented in several global
databases, the pipeline uses the maximum configured population AF rather than a
single database.

# Configuration file

Most behavior is controlled by a YAML file. A minimal example:

```yaml
input:
  variants: "data/demo/variants.tsv"
  delimiter: "\t"
  sample_metadata: "data/demo/sample_metadata.tsv"
  metadata_delimiter: "\t"

output:
  dir: "results/demo_tumor_only_filter"

hard_filters:
  enabled: true
  assay: "WGS"
  adaptive_depth_fraction: 0.35
  adaptive_q25_depth_fraction: 0.50
  missing_required_metrics_fail: false

population_filters:
  common_af: 0.005
  rare_af: 0.001
  use_population_columns:
    - GMAF
    - gnomADe_AF
    - gnomADg_AF
    - ABraOM_AF
    - BIPMed_AF

validation:
  oncokb: "data/demo/oncokb.tsv"
  cosmic: "data/demo/cosmic.tsv"

tmb:
  callable_mb: 30

clonality:
  enabled: true
  clonal_ccf_cutoff: 0.85
  subclonal_ccf_cutoff: 0.55

visualization:
  enabled: true
  oncoplot_top_genes: 20
```

Important fields to review before running real cohorts:

| Field | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| `hard_filters.assay` | Uses assay-aware defaults for WGS, WES or panel data. |
| `tmb.callable_mb` | Must match the callable territory of the assay. |
| `population_filters.use_population_columns` | Should include local Brazilian resources when available. |
| `validation.oncokb` and `validation.cosmic` | Used for validation/prioritization, not as sole proof of somatic origin. |
| `driver_resources.driver_genes` | Allows tumor-type-specific driver interpretation. |
| `clonality` | CCF requires purity and copy number for stronger interpretation. |

# Running the workflow

From the repository root:

```{bash run-demo, eval=FALSE}
Rscript scripts/run_automated_variant_analysis.R --config config/demo_config.yml
```

The older entry point is equivalent:

```{bash run-demo-alt, eval=FALSE}
Rscript scripts/run_tumor_only_filter.R --config config/demo_config.yml
```

For the 100k-row test subset:

```{bash run-subset, eval=FALSE}
Rscript scripts/run_automated_variant_analysis.R --config config/wgs_all_patients_subset_config.yml
```

For the full WGS file:

```{bash run-full, eval=FALSE}
Rscript scripts/run_automated_variant_analysis.R --config config/wgs_all_patients_config.yml
```

The full WGS run can be memory and time intensive because the current
implementation reads the complete table into memory.

# Output structure

After a run, the output directory contains:

```{r list-demo-output}
outdir <- "results/demo_tumor_only_filter"
if (dir.exists(outdir)) {
  sort(list.files(outdir))
} else {
  message("Run the demo first to populate ", outdir)
}
```

The most important files are:

| File | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `variants_scored.tsv` | Main per-variant output with scores, classes, ML and clonality fields. |
| `variants_high_confidence_somatic.tsv` | Strict subset of high-confidence tumor-only somatic candidates. |
| `variants_uncertain_for_review.tsv` | Variants that should be reviewed manually or in IGV. |
| `removed_probable_germline.tsv` | Variants more compatible with germline polymorphism. |
| `removed_probable_artifact.tsv` | Variants more compatible with technical artifact. |
| `sample_qc_summary.tsv` | Sample-level technical QC and adaptive thresholds. |
| `variant_call_qc_summary.tsv` | Per-sample classification counts and technical metrics. |
| `ml_filter_metrics.tsv` | Whether pan-cancer and tumor-specific ML models trained and label counts. |
| `ml_training_labels.tsv` | Audit table of manual and pseudo-labels used for training. |
| `active_learning_candidates.tsv` | Variants prioritized for the next manual review cycle. |
| `tmb_summary.tsv` | TMB per sample. |
| `clonality_summary.tsv` | Approximate clonal/subclonal summary per sample. |
| `driver_classification_summary.tsv` | Driver/passenger class counts. |
| `figure_manifest.tsv` | List of generated figures and skipped optional plots. |

# Understanding `final_class`

`final_class` is the main tumor-only filtering result:

| Class | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| `high_confidence_somatic` | Strong technical and somatic evidence, often with validation support. |
| `probable_somatic` | Good tumor-only somatic candidate, but less supported than high confidence. |
| `uncertain` | Cannot confidently classify; review manually. |
| `probable_germline` | Compatible with germline polymorphism or population recurrence. |
| `probable_artifact` | Compatible with technical artifact. |
| `hard_filter_fail` | Failed required technical/sample-level filters. |

Read the final class together with:

| Column | Use |
|---|---|
| `somatic_score` | Strength of somatic evidence. |
| `germline_score` | Evidence for polymorphism/germline origin. |
| `artifact_score` | Evidence for technical artifact. |
| `hard_filter_reason` | Which hard criteria failed. |
| `variant_cohort_freq` | Internal recurrence across samples. |
| `variant_tumor_type_freq` | Recurrence within tumor type. |
| `max_pop_af` | Maximum AF across configured population databases. |

Example:

```{r inspect-final-class}
variants_file <- file.path(outdir, "variants_scored.tsv")
if (file.exists(variants_file)) {
  x <- data.table::fread(variants_file)
  cols <- intersect(
    c("sample_id", "gene", "protein_change", "final_class",
      "somatic_score", "germline_score", "artifact_score",
      "max_pop_af", "variant_cohort_freq", "hard_filter_reason"),
    names(x)
  )
  head(x[, ..cols])
}
```

# Sample and variant-call QC

The QC outputs should be reviewed before interpreting biology.

```{r sample-qc}
sample_qc_file <- file.path(outdir, "sample_qc_summary.tsv")
if (file.exists(sample_qc_file)) {
  data.table::fread(sample_qc_file)
}
```

Important signals:

| Metric | Concern if high/low |
|---|---|
| `sample_variant_median_dp` | Low median depth reduces sensitivity and can inflate false negatives. |
| `sample_missing_dp_fraction` | High missingness means input lost technical information. |
| `sample_qc_class` | `fail` means variants from that sample should be treated cautiously. |
| `external_contamination` | High contamination increases false positives and VAF distortion. |
| `external_duplicate_rate` | High duplication reduces independent evidence. |

Variant-call QC summarizes how many variants were removed or retained per
sample:

```{r variant-qc}
variant_qc_file <- file.path(outdir, "variant_call_qc_summary.tsv")
if (file.exists(variant_qc_file)) {
  data.table::fread(variant_qc_file)
}
```

# OncoKB and COSMIC validation layer

OncoKB and COSMIC are used as supporting evidence. A match can rescue or
prioritize a variant, but it does not override low depth, poor mapping, high
population frequency or likely artifact evidence.

Useful columns:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `oncokb_match` | Matched OncoKB record by coordinate or gene/protein change. |
| `cosmic_match` | Matched COSMIC record or COSMIC identifier. |
| `validation_rescue_candidate` | Variant worth manual review despite borderline score. |
| `oncokb_oncogenic` | OncoKB oncogenic annotation when available. |
| `cosmic_count` | Recurrence count in COSMIC when available. |

```{r validation-summary}
validation_file <- file.path(outdir, "oncokb_cosmic_validation_summary.tsv")
if (file.exists(validation_file)) {
  data.table::fread(validation_file)
}
```

# Incremental ML filter

The machine-learning layer is incremental. It can start with pseudo-labels and
then improve as manually reviewed variants are added.

The model uses two label sources:

| Source | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `manual_review` | Curated labels supplied in `ml_filter.review_labels`. |
| `pseudo_rules` | High-confidence labels derived from technical, population and validation rules. |

Manual labels receive higher weight than pseudo-labels. This allows the model
to improve gradually without discarding the conservative tumor-only rules.

Example review label file:

```text
sample_id	tumor_type	chrom	pos	ref	alt	gene	protein_change	reviewed_label	evidence	reviewer	review_date	notes
S3	UCEC	7	140453136	A	T	BRAF	p.V600E	true_somatic	IGV_hotspot	MB	2026-06-21	adequate depth and VAF
S4	UCEC	7	140453136	A	T	BRAF	p.V600E	artifact	low_depth_low_quality	MB	2026-06-21	technical failure
```

Accepted positive labels include `true_somatic`, `validated_somatic`,
`true_positive` and `TP`. Accepted negative labels include `false_positive`,
`artifact`, `germline`, `polymorphism`, `technical` and `not_somatic`.
Uncertain labels are kept for documentation but are not used for training.

When enough labels exist, the pipeline trains:

| Model | Use |
|---|---|
| `glm_PANCANCER` | Global model trained across tumor types. |
| `glm_<TUMOR_TYPE>` | Tumor-specific model, for example `glm_UCEC`. |

Prediction uses the tumor-specific model first. If no tumor-specific model is
available, it falls back to the pan-cancer model.

```{r ml-summary}
ml_file <- file.path(outdir, "ml_filter_metrics.tsv")
if (file.exists(ml_file)) {
  data.table::fread(ml_file)
}
```

Interpretation:

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| `trained` | Use `ml_true_positive_probability` as an additional prioritization signal. |
| `insufficient_training_labels` | Add reviewed positives and negatives, stronger validation resources, or more samples. |
| `disabled` | ML was not requested in the config. |

Useful ML output columns:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `ml_label` | Training label used by the model. |
| `ml_label_source` | `manual_review` or `pseudo_rules`. |
| `ml_true_positive_probability` | Final ML probability used by the pipeline. |
| `ml_pancancer_true_positive_probability` | Probability from the pan-cancer model. |
| `ml_tumor_type_true_positive_probability` | Probability from the tumor-specific model. |
| `ml_model_scope` | Whether the final prediction used tumor-specific or pan-cancer model. |
| `ml_model_id` | Model identifier. |

The pipeline also writes `ml_training_labels.tsv`, an audit table of all labels
used for training.

```{r ml-training-labels}
training_labels_file <- file.path(outdir, "ml_training_labels.tsv")
if (file.exists(training_labels_file)) {
  head(data.table::fread(training_labels_file))
}
```

Active learning candidates are written to `active_learning_candidates.tsv`.
These are the variants the model/rules think would be most useful to review in
the next cycle.

```{r active-learning}
active_file <- file.path(outdir, "active_learning_candidates.tsv")
if (file.exists(active_file)) {
  head(data.table::fread(active_file))
}
```

Recommended cycle:

```text
run pipeline -> review active_learning_candidates.tsv ->
update review_labels.tsv -> rerun pipeline
```

Do not use ML probability alone to call a variant somatic. It should support,
not replace, hard filters, biological interpretation, matched normal data when
available, and manual review.

# TMB

TMB is computed from `high_confidence_somatic` and `probable_somatic` variants
with countable coding/splice consequences.

```{r tmb-summary}
tmb_file <- file.path(outdir, "tmb_summary.tsv")
if (file.exists(tmb_file)) {
  data.table::fread(tmb_file)
}
```

The most common error in TMB is an incorrect denominator. Review
`tmb.callable_mb` for each assay:

| Assay | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| WES | Use the callable coding territory of the exome/capture. |
| Panel | Use the true panel territory in Mb. |
| WGS | Decide explicitly whether reporting coding-like TMB or genome-wide burden. |

# Driver and passenger classification

Driver classification is downstream of somatic filtering. A variant likely to
be germline or artifact is not promoted to driver simply because it is in a
cancer gene.

```{r driver-summary}
driver_file <- file.path(outdir, "driver_classification_summary.tsv")
if (file.exists(driver_file)) {
  data.table::fread(driver_file)
}
```

Classes:

| `driver_class` | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `known_driver` | Strong curated support, hotspot/OncoKB/COSMIC evidence. |
| `probable_driver` | Gene, consequence and functional evidence are compatible. |
| `possible_driver` | Partial evidence, requires review. |
| `likely_passenger` | Somatic candidate without driver evidence. |
| `not_evaluable_as_driver` | Not a reliable somatic candidate. |

The workflow also writes:

| File | Use |
|---|---|
| `dndscv_input.tsv` | Input for dNdScv positive-selection analysis. |
| `chasmplus_missense_input.tsv` | Input for CHASMplus-like driver/passenger scoring. |

# Clonality

The pipeline estimates cancer cell fraction (CCF) when possible:

```text
CCF = VAF * (purity * total_cn + (1 - purity) * normal_cn) /
      (purity * mutation_multiplicity)
```

Recognized columns:

| Concept | Accepted examples |
|---|---|
| Purity | `tumor_purity`, `purity`, `PURITY`, `Tumor_Purity` |
| Total copy number | `total_cn`, `Total_CN`, `CN`, `copy_number`, `local_cn`, `tcn` |
| Mutation multiplicity | `multiplicity`, `mutation_multiplicity`, `mut_cn`, `mutation_cn` |

```{r clonality-summary}
clonality_file <- file.path(outdir, "clonality_summary.tsv")
if (file.exists(clonality_file)) {
  data.table::fread(clonality_file)
}
```

Interpretation:

| Method | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `purity_copy_number_adjusted` | Best internal estimate: purity and local CN were available. |
| `purity_adjusted_copy_neutral` | Purity was available, but CN was assumed to be 2. |
| `vaf_proxy_no_purity` | No formal CCF; clonal class is based on VAF only. |

For definitive clonal architecture, integrate this output with dedicated
purity/CNV tools such as ABSOLUTE, FACETS, Sequenza, PURPLE or ichorCNA.

# Figures

The pipeline writes figures under `figures/` and records all generated or
skipped plots in `figure_manifest.tsv`.

```{r figure-manifest}
fig_file <- file.path(outdir, "figure_manifest.tsv")
if (file.exists(fig_file)) {
  data.table::fread(fig_file)
}
```

Main figures:

| Figure | Use |
|---|---|
| `oncoplot_fallback_top_genes.png` | Top mutated genes by sample. |
| `maftools_oncoplot_top_genes.pdf` | Native maftools oncoplot, if maftools is installed. |
| `variant_classification_stack.png` | Counts of final classes per sample. |
| `tmb_by_sample.png` | TMB distribution and thresholds. |
| `driver_class_by_sample.png` | Driver/passenger categories per sample. |
| `qc_vaf_histogram_by_class.png` | VAF distribution by final class. |
| `qc_depth_histogram_by_class.png` | Depth distribution by final class. |
| `recurrence_vs_population_af.png` | Internal recurrence vs population AF. |
| `clonality_class_by_sample.png` | Clonal/subclonal count per sample. |
| `clonality_ccf_or_vaf_distribution.png` | CCF or VAF proxy distribution. |

Example display in an interactive R Markdown run:

```{r show-oncoplot, eval=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(outdir, "figures", "oncoplot_fallback_top_genes.png"))
```

# Applying to multiple tumor types

The workflow can be run as pan-cancer or tumor-type-aware. For better
interpretation:

1. Provide `tumor_type` in the input table or `input.sample_metadata`.
2. Use OncoTree-like codes when possible, such as `UCEC`, `BRCA`, `LUAD`,
   `COADREAD`.
3. Provide tumor-type-specific driver gene and hotspot references when
   available.
4. Review both pan-cohort recurrence and within-tumor-type recurrence.

Example sample metadata:

```text
sample_id	tumor_type	assay	tumor_purity
S1	UCEC	WGS	0.60
S2	BRCA	WES	0.45
S3	LUAD	WGS	0.70
```

# Recommended manual review

After the first run, manually review a balanced set in IGV or another read
viewer:

| Set | Why |
|---|---|
| 50 `high_confidence_somatic` | Estimate false-positive rate among retained calls. |
| 50 `probable_germline` | Confirm population/germline filter behavior. |
| 50 `probable_artifact` | Confirm technical artifact patterns. |
| 50 `uncertain` | Tune thresholds and rescue rules. |
| All `validation_rescue_candidate` | Curated databases may reveal true positives near thresholds. |
| Recurrent same-position variants | Distinguish hotspot biology from cohort artifact/polymorphism. |

# Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Likely cause | Suggested action |
|---|---|---|
| Many variants retained at VAF near 0.5 | Germline variants retained in tumor-only mode. | Add local population AF, internal recurrence and matched normals when possible. |
| Many recurrent variants absent from COSMIC/OncoKB | Polymorphism, mapping artifact or recurrent technical error. | Review cohort frequency, population AF, low-complexity regions and IGV. |
| `ml_filter_metrics.tsv` says `insufficient_training_labels` | Not enough positive/negative manual or pseudo-labels. | Review `active_learning_candidates.tsv`, add curated labels, OncoKB/COSMIC/hotspots or more samples. |
| TMB is implausibly high | Wrong callable territory or germline/artifact leakage. | Review `tmb.callable_mb` and filtered classes. |
| Clonality is `vaf_proxy_no_purity` | Purity was not supplied. | Add purity and local CN columns from a CNV/purity tool. |
| maftools plots skipped | `maftools` is not installed. | Install with `BiocManager::install("maftools")`. |

# Reproducibility

Record the exact config file, input variant table, reference files and package
versions for every cohort. The config file is part of the analysis result, not
just a launch parameter.

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
